notes: Each eval drives the axon4to5-migrate Skill with the interceptors recipe in single mode. Coverage: dispatch interceptor (Spring Boot Path A), handler interceptor with lifecycle hooks (Spring Boot Path A), handler interceptor with native registration-site rewrite (Path B), annotation-based @MessageHandlerInterceptor (Blocker B1), Rejected (event-handler class routed away). Fixtures: axon4/bikerental/ for the real workshop source, synthetic/ for constructed cases.

evals.name: spring-axon-dispatch-interceptor
evals.prompt:
You are running eval `1: spring-axon-dispatch-interceptor` for the `axon4to5-migrate` Skill. The Skill lives at `$SKILL_DIR`. Load `$SKILL_DIR/SKILL.md`, `$SKILL_DIR/patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`, and (if you need fuller before/after walkthroughs) `$SKILL_DIR/examples/ALL_EXAMPLES.md`.

**Skip SKILL.md Step 1** (the Q1/Q2/Q3 AskUserQuestion prompts) — pin these answers directly: `configuration=spring`, Migration approach **B (AI only)**, `skip-openrewrite=true` (the harness has no Maven/Gradle project and you cannot invoke another Skill recursively). **Skip Step 2.5** progress tracking — there is no project root, just a single workspace directory.

The migration target is the file at `$SOURCE`. Its parent directory `$WORKSPACE/outputs/` is the working directory; treat any `secondary_fixtures` siblings already staged there as additional in-scope files. Migrate every target file IN PLACE.

**Identify the AF4 shapes in `$SOURCE` against `patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`** and apply the matching pattern(s). The patterns this eval exercises are: `patterns/50-interceptors/message-dispatch-interceptor.md`. Reach for `examples/interceptors/01-dispatch-spring-boot.md` when you need a full before/after walkthrough.

**Eval-specific guidance:** DispatchTimeCommandDispatchInterceptor implements MessageDispatchInterceptor<CommandMessage<?>> with AF4 `BiFunction<Integer, CommandMessage<?>, CommandMessage<?>> handle(List<? extends CommandMessage<?>> messages)` returning a lambda that calls `command.withMetaData(Map.of(...))`. Apply the matching patterns: rewrite to `MessageStream<?> interceptOnDispatch(CommandMessage message, @Nullable ProcessingContext context, MessageDispatchInterceptorChain<CommandMessage> chain)` — collapse the batch-lambda body inline, call `message.andMetadata(Collections.singletonMap(...))`, return `chain.proceed(enrichedMessage, context)`. Generic type: `CommandMessage<?>` → `CommandMessage` (no wildcard). Remove AF4 imports: `org.axonframework.commandhandling.CommandMessage`, `org.axonframework.messaging.MessageDispatchInterceptor`, `java.util.List`, `java.util.function.BiFunction`, `java.util.Map`. Add AF5 imports: `org.axonframework.messaging.commandhandling.CommandMessage`, `org.axonframework.messaging.core.MessageDispatchInterceptor`, `org.axonframework.messaging.core.MessageDispatchInterceptorChain`, `org.axonframework.messaging.core.MessageStream`, `org.axonframework.messaging.core.unitofwork.ProcessingContext`, `org.jspecify.annotations.Nullable`, `java.util.Collections`. No @Component on this class — it has no Spring stereotype (check fixture).

Write the `**Result:**` block (per SKILL.md Step 4d) to `$WORKSPACE/outputs/result.md`. Required format: `**Result:** ✅ Success`, followed by `**Recipe:** axon4to5-interceptors` and a `**Notes:**` line summarising what was changed.
evals.expected_output: Dispatch interceptor rewritten to AF5 interceptOnDispatch signature; Result: ✅ Success.
expectations: Result block marks Success with axon4to5-interceptors

evals.name: spring-axon-handler-interceptor-lifecycle-hooks
evals.prompt:
You are running eval `2: spring-axon-handler-interceptor-lifecycle-hooks` for the `axon4to5-migrate` Skill. The Skill lives at `$SKILL_DIR`. Load `$SKILL_DIR/SKILL.md`, `$SKILL_DIR/patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`, and (if you need fuller before/after walkthroughs) `$SKILL_DIR/examples/ALL_EXAMPLES.md`.

**Skip SKILL.md Step 1** (the Q1/Q2/Q3 AskUserQuestion prompts) — pin these answers directly: `configuration=spring`, Migration approach **B (AI only)**, `skip-openrewrite=true` (the harness has no Maven/Gradle project and you cannot invoke another Skill recursively). **Skip Step 2.5** progress tracking — there is no project root, just a single workspace directory.

The migration target is the file at `$SOURCE`. Its parent directory `$WORKSPACE/outputs/` is the working directory; treat any `secondary_fixtures` siblings already staged there as additional in-scope files. Migrate every target file IN PLACE.

**Identify the AF4 shapes in `$SOURCE` against `patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`** and apply the matching pattern(s). The patterns this eval exercises are: `patterns/50-interceptors/message-handler-interceptor.md`. Reach for `examples/interceptors/02-handler-spring-boot.md` when you need a full before/after walkthrough.

**Eval-specific guidance:** LoggingCommandHandlerInterceptor implements MessageHandlerInterceptor<CommandMessage<?>> with AF4 `Object handle(UnitOfWork<? extends CommandMessage<?>> unitOfWork, InterceptorChain interceptorChain) throws Exception`. Body uses: `unitOfWork.getMessage()`, `unitOfWork.onCommit(uow -> {...})`, `unitOfWork.onRollback(uow -> {...})`, returns `interceptorChain.proceed()`. Apply the matching patterns: rewrite to `MessageStream<?> interceptOnHandle(CommandMessage message, ProcessingContext context, MessageHandlerInterceptorChain<CommandMessage> chain)` — use `message` param directly (drop `unitOfWork.getMessage()`); `unitOfWork.onCommit(...)` → `context.runOnAfterCommit(ctx -> {...})`; `unitOfWork.onRollback(...)` → `context.onError((ctx, error) -> {...})`; `return interceptorChain.proceed()` → `return chain.proceed(message, context)`. Remove `throws Exception`. Generic: `CommandMessage<?>` → `CommandMessage`. Remove AF4 imports: `org.axonframework.commandhandling.CommandMessage`, `org.axonframework.messaging.InterceptorChain`, `org.axonframework.messaging.MessageHandlerInterceptor`, `org.axonframework.messaging.unitofwork.UnitOfWork`. Add AF5 imports: `org.axonframework.messaging.commandhandling.CommandMessage`, `org.axonframework.messaging.core.MessageHandlerInterceptor`, `org.axonframework.messaging.core.MessageHandlerInterceptorChain`, `org.axonframework.messaging.core.MessageStream`, `org.axonframework.messaging.core.unitofwork.ProcessingContext`. @Component preserved.

Write the `**Result:**` block (per SKILL.md Step 4d) to `$WORKSPACE/outputs/result.md`. Required format: `**Result:** ✅ Success`, followed by `**Recipe:** axon4to5-interceptors` and a `**Notes:**` line summarising what was changed.
evals.expected_output: Handler interceptor rewritten to AF5 interceptOnHandle with lifecycle hooks migrated; Result: ✅ Success.
expectations: ProcessingContext context parameter (no @Nullable — handler context is mandatory)
onError present with two-arg lambda (was onRollback)
Result block marks Success with axon4to5-interceptors

evals.name: native-axon-handler-interceptor-with-registration
evals.prompt:
You are running eval `3: native-axon-handler-interceptor-with-registration` for the `axon4to5-migrate` Skill. The Skill lives at `$SKILL_DIR`. Load `$SKILL_DIR/SKILL.md`, `$SKILL_DIR/patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`, and (if you need fuller before/after walkthroughs) `$SKILL_DIR/examples/ALL_EXAMPLES.md`.

**Skip SKILL.md Step 1** (the Q1/Q2/Q3 AskUserQuestion prompts) — pin these answers directly: `configuration=native`, Migration approach **B (AI only)**, `skip-openrewrite=true` (the harness has no Maven/Gradle project and you cannot invoke another Skill recursively). **Skip Step 2.5** progress tracking — there is no project root, just a single workspace directory.

The migration target is the file at `$SOURCE`. Its parent directory `$WORKSPACE/outputs/` is the working directory; treat any `secondary_fixtures` siblings already staged there as additional in-scope files. Migrate every target file IN PLACE.

**Identify the AF4 shapes in `$SOURCE` against `patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`** and apply the matching pattern(s). The patterns this eval exercises are: `patterns/50-interceptors/message-handler-interceptor.md`. Reach for `examples/interceptors/03-handler-native-config.md` when you need a full before/after walkthrough.

**Eval-specific guidance:** Two files in scope: (1) AuditCommandHandlerInterceptor.java — implements MessageHandlerInterceptor<CommandMessage<?>> with AF4 `Object handle(UnitOfWork<? extends CommandMessage<?>> unitOfWork, InterceptorChain interceptorChain) throws Exception`. Uses `unitOfWork.getMessage()`, `unitOfWork.onPrepareCommit(uow -> {...})`, `unitOfWork.onCommit(uow -> {...})`, `Object result = interceptorChain.proceed()`. (2) AuditInterceptorNativeConfig.java — has `Configurer configurer = DefaultConfigurer.defaultConfiguration()` and `configurer.registerCommandHandlerInterceptor(config -> new AuditCommandHandlerInterceptor())`. Apply the matching patterns: rewrite AuditCommandHandlerInterceptor to `MessageStream<?> interceptOnHandle(CommandMessage message, ProcessingContext context, MessageHandlerInterceptorChain<CommandMessage> chain)` — `unitOfWork.getMessage()` gone (use message), `onPrepareCommit` → `context.runOnPreInvocation(ctx -> {...})`, `onCommit` → `context.runOnAfterCommit(ctx -> {...})`, `return chain.proceed(message, context)` (result variable gone — async). In AuditInterceptorNativeConfig: `Configurer` → `MessagingConfigurer`, `DefaultConfigurer.defaultConfiguration()` → `MessagingConfigurer.create()`, import `org.axonframework.messaging.core.config.MessagingConfigurer`. Method name `registerCommandHandlerInterceptor` unchanged. Remove `org.axonframework.config.Configurer`, `org.axonframework.config.DefaultConfigurer` imports.

Write the `**Result:**` block (per SKILL.md Step 4d) to `$WORKSPACE/outputs/result.md`. Required format: `**Result:** ✅ Success`, followed by `**Recipe:** axon4to5-interceptors` and a `**Notes:**` line summarising what was changed.
evals.expected_output: Handler interceptor rewritten to AF5; config registration receiver changed to MessagingConfigurer; Result: ✅ Success.
expectations: AF4 Configurer import removed from config
AF4 DefaultConfigurer import removed from config
Result block marks Success with axon4to5-interceptors

assertions.text: AF4 UnitOfWork import removed from interceptor
AF4 InterceptorChain import removed from interceptor
AF5 MessagingConfigurer import added to config
AF4 Configurer import removed from config

evals.name: spring-axon-annotation-interceptor-blocker
evals.prompt:
You are running eval `4: spring-axon-annotation-interceptor-blocker` for the `axon4to5-migrate` Skill. The Skill lives at `$SKILL_DIR`. Load `$SKILL_DIR/SKILL.md`, `$SKILL_DIR/patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`, and (if you need fuller before/after walkthroughs) `$SKILL_DIR/examples/ALL_EXAMPLES.md`.

**Skip SKILL.md Step 1** (the Q1/Q2/Q3 AskUserQuestion prompts) — pin these answers directly: `configuration=spring`, Migration approach **B (AI only)**, `skip-openrewrite=true` (the harness has no Maven/Gradle project and you cannot invoke another Skill recursively). **Skip Step 2.5** progress tracking — there is no project root, just a single workspace directory.

The migration target is the file at `$SOURCE`. Its parent directory `$WORKSPACE/outputs/` is the working directory; treat any `secondary_fixtures` siblings already staged there as additional in-scope files. Migrate every target file IN PLACE.

**Identify the AF4 shapes in `$SOURCE` against `patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`** and apply the matching pattern(s). The patterns this eval exercises are: `patterns/50-interceptors/message-handler-interceptor.md`. Reach for `examples/interceptors/04-annotation-blocker.md` when you need a full before/after walkthrough.

**Eval-specific guidance:** AnnotationInterceptorHandler has a `@CommandHandler` method AND a method annotated with `@MessageHandlerInterceptor(messageType = CommandMessage.class)` — the annotation form, NOT implementing the `MessageHandlerInterceptor<M>` interface. Per `patterns/50-interceptors/message-handler-interceptor.md`, the `@MessageHandlerInterceptor` method-annotation form has no AF5 equivalent — Blocker B1. Detect it as a method annotation and emit the blocker. Do NOT edit the source file.

This eval expects a **Blocker** outcome. The fixture contains an AF4 shape that has no AF5 equivalent (e.g. `@DeadlineHandler`, `SagaTestFixture`, `snapshotTriggerDefinition`, Map-typed `@AggregateMember`, `MongoEventStorageEngine`, the `@MessageHandlerInterceptor` method-annotation form). Comment out the blocking code, add a `// TODO: no AF5 equivalent` note, and surface the blocker in the Result block.

Do NOT edit the source file beyond what SKILL.md Step 2 prescribes for blockers (comment out + `// TODO: no AF5 equivalent`). Write the `**Result:**` block to `$WORKSPACE/outputs/result.md` with `**Result:** 🚧 Blocker`, `**Recipe:** axon4to5-interceptors`, a `**Notes:**` line naming the blocker, and an `**Options:**` list enumerating `skip`, `revert`, `solve-manually`.
evals.expected_output: B1 Blocker emitted; source untouched; Result: 🚧 Blocker.
expectations: Notes cite file:line of the annotation

**Identify the AF4 shapes in `$SOURCE` against `patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`** and apply the matching pattern(s). The patterns this eval exercises are: `patterns/50-interceptors/message-handler-interceptor.md`. Reach for `examples/event-handlers/01-pure-projector-spring.md` when you need a full before/after walkthrough.

**Eval-specific guidance:** DwellingReadModelProjector has @ProcessingGroup and @EventHandler methods — it is a projector, NOT an interceptor. The interceptor patterns do not apply: there is no `MessageDispatchInterceptor` / `MessageHandlerInterceptor` interface implementation and no `@MessageHandlerInterceptor` method annotation — Reject. Source MUST stay byte-identical — no @Namespace, no AF5 imports, no interceptOnDispatch/interceptOnHandle added.

This eval expects a **Rejected** outcome. The file is out of scope for the `interceptors` pattern set (e.g. a projector when this recipe targets aggregates, an aggregate when this recipe targets sagas). Do not migrate it — record Rejected in the Result block.

The source must NOT be edited — no patterns from this recipe apply. Write the `**Result:**` block to `$WORKSPACE/outputs/result.md` with `**Result:** ⏭️ Rejected`, `**Recipe:** axon4to5-interceptors`, and a `**Notes:**` line explaining why this file is not in scope for the interceptors pattern set.
evals.expected_output: Projector rejected; source untouched; Result: ⏭️ Rejected.
expectations: Notes name failed predicate (no interceptor interface)
Notes suggest routing to event-processor recipe
